questions.question: She ___ completed all her training by next month.
They'll have ___ together for 25 years next June.
By midnight, we'll have ___ over 500 kilometres.
Will you have ___ the project by the time I return?
He'll have ___ by the time his youngest child starts school.
By the end of this week, I'll have ___ enough for the ticket.
The builders will have ___ the bridge by spring.
___ they have left by the time we arrive?
By next week, she'll have ___ all the thank-you cards.
I'll have ___ here for two years next March.
He'll have ___ the whole series before the weekend.
By the time the project ends, the team ___ worked together for three years.
She will have ___ over 10,000 km by the end of the race.
Will the students have ___ all the chapters by Friday?
By the time he's 40, he'll have ___ five novels.
I won't ___ submitted my application before the deadline.
By next semester, they'll have ___ over 500 new words.
She will have ___ breakfast before the meeting starts.
By the time you read this, I'll ___ already gone home.
He will have ___ all his exams by the end of June.

theory.explanation:
Future Perfect tense

Form: will have + past participle (3rd form of the verb)
Short forms: I'll have, she'll have...

When we use it:
We describe an action that will be completed BEFORE a particular moment in the future.

Typical time expressions:
  by next year, by the time, by 2030, by Friday, before the meeting

Patterns:
  Affirmative: subject + will have + pp
  Negative:    subject + won't have + pp
  Questions:   Will + subject + have + pp?

examples.sentence: She will have completed all her training by next month.
examples.explanation: Affirmative: will have + completed. By next month = before next month.

examples.sentence: I won't have submitted my application before the deadline.
examples.explanation: Negative: won't have + submitted. The action will not be done on time.

examples.sentence: Will they have left by the time we arrive?
examples.explanation: Question: Will + they + have + left? Will they leave before we arrive?
